key_insight: HACO is a human-AI co-discovery system that transferred MaskGIT from vision to crystal structure prediction, then refined it with sparse human guidance into MaskGXT.
engineering_relevance: The work suggests AI-assisted cross-domain search can accelerate discovery of better generative models for materials design and crystal structure prediction.
method: Searches across generative modeling methods from multiple fields to find transferable ideas
Instantiates MaskGIT as a discrete-token crystal structure model
Adds symmetry tokens, space-group-stratified sampling, and sub-bin coordinate refinement
evidence_signal: On the MP-20 polymorph split, MaskGXT achieved 79.06% METRe accuracy versus 70.87% for the strongest baseline, and it also led on standard MP-20 and MPTS-52 benchmarks.
watch_item: Results depend on well-aligned, low-cost validation settings, so transferability to harder or less clearly evaluable scientific domains may be limited.
paper_title: Discovering Crystal Structure Prediction Algorithms with an AI Co-Scientist
